_comment: Community projects: people building things for Burlington. To add one: copy any entry and append. maker is optional (null hides the credit line).

projects.name: Front Porch Forum
projects.what: Born in a Burlington neighborhood twenty years ago, this human-moderated email forum now helps a quarter-million Vermont households swap lost-dog notices, mechanic tips, and small kindnesses every day.

projects.name: Seven Days
projects.what: Vermont's independent alt-weekly has chronicled Burlington's food, music, politics, and people since 1995 — and its calendar remains the definitive answer to what's happening this week.

projects.name: VTDigger
projects.what: The nonprofit newsroom whose Daily Digger email keeps tens of thousands of Vermonters up to speed on statehouse and local news, digging for the truth since 2009.

projects.name: BTV Daily
projects.what: A free every-morning email that gathers Burlington's weather, headlines, events, and the best of local social media into one quick read.

projects.name: Good Govermont
projects.what: Nathaniel Eisen's Substack digs into land use, transit, and environmental policy with a sharp focus on Burlington and the rest of Chittenden County.

projects.name: The Winooski News
projects.what: Student reporters paired with professional editors cover the Onion City's people and happenings for free, through UVM's Community News Service.

projects.name: WBTV-LP 99.3 FM
projects.what: Burlington's all-volunteer low-power community radio station, where neighbors train each other to make radio and broadcast 24 hours a day from Flynn Avenue.
projects.maker: WBTV-LP volunteers at the Media Factory

projects.name: Rumble Strip
projects.what: Erica Heilman records uncommon, deeply human conversations with everyday Vermonters from her home in East Calais — one of the most honored independent podcasts anywhere.

projects.name: Vermont Talks
projects.what: Software engineer Becca Hammond sits down for long, generous interviews with Burlington-area artists, musicians, and business owners, asking "What's new, 802?"

projects.name: Brave Little State
projects.what: Vermont Public's people-powered podcast lets listeners ask and vote on the questions, then brings them along to report the answers about life in Vermont.

projects.name: ORCA — Open Research Community Accelerator
projects.what: UVM student pods build open-source tools for their neighbors — including BTV Alerts for Burlington residents and the statewide Vermont Zoning Atlas mapping all 237 towns.

projects.name: Big Heavy World
projects.what: A volunteer crew has spent nearly three decades preserving Vermont-made music — an archive of close to 5,000 recordings and a megaphone for local musicians.

projects.name: The Media Factory
projects.what: Burlington's community media center hands anyone free cameras, studios, training, and airtime — proof that local storytelling belongs to everyone.

projects.name: Preservation Burlington
projects.what: Volunteer-led walking tours of Church Street, Pine Street, and Lakeview Cemetery — cider and donuts included — plus year-round advocacy for the buildings that give Burlington its character.

projects.name: Chittenden County Historical Society
projects.what: An all-volunteer society founded in 1965 that researches, publishes, and celebrates the history of Burlington and the Champlain Valley.
